## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hi, this is Don Crowder with an overview of the conversion campaign that you're going to be running after your webinar. So most people run a webinar and they're happy to walk out with whatever sales they get from it. But in a well-designed webinar funnel, 50 to 70% of the webinar campaign sales are going to happen outside of the webinar. So if you decide to just run a webinar and then walk away with whatever sales you get from that, you're potentially missing out on 50 to 70% of the sales you could be getting from that campaign by putting together this post-webinar sales campaign. There are three audiences. I'll be talking to you about that in a separate video. But if you look at the foundational one that I'm going to show you demonstrate all this on, let's call this our internal promo sequence. So this is the group of people where you already have their email. Either they're already on your list and you pitch the webinar to them or you come in with, you introduce them to you through an ad that they then opt in. And then after they opt in, then they are introduced to the webinar. And so one way or another, you've got their email address before you invite them to register for the webinar. So it starts with a mailing to your list. From there, they're offered the chance to register for the webinar. If they say yes, then you send them a series of emails that we talked about in a previous module where we talk about coming to the webinar. So this is called the attendance promotion. And the objective of the attendance promotion is to get them to actually show up on the webinar. Now, the next question is, did they attend the webinar? If they attended, did they buy? If they bought, then you take them to a checkout page and then assuming they check out properly, then you take them to product delivery of your product. And then when that once that product is entirely done, you then re nurture them by helping them by giving them some more free content that will enable them to feel positively about you that you're not going to just pitch them on everything that you sell them. And then you're going to move into your next offer sequence. Now, going back to the webinar again. So a second option when they is when they come to a webinar is that they attended, but they didn't buy. And so then we take them through what we call a replay promo where we are promoting the replay recordings of the webinar.

## [03:01 - 05:38]

They then when they watch those, they can buy, which takes them back through our checkout and product delivery sequence. But if they don't buy from the replay promo, then we take them into another process called the sales page promo. So we have a separate sales page, which is different than the abbreviated sales page that they saw on the webinar and on the replay. This is a full sales page that gives them all the information they need to be able to buy. And so then as they come to that, if they buy, then we take them through the checkout and the product delivery stages. If they didn't buy, then basically we're done with this particular webinar campaign. And so we take them back into a re nurture campaign and then into the next offer sequence. All right, now let's go back to this stage. What happens if they're one of the 70% of people who don't register for your webinar? What happens then? Well, what we then do is we take those people who didn't register for the webinar. Remember, we have their email address. We they didn't register for the webinar and we take them and we show them the replay promotion, which then takes them through all the other steps of whether they bought or not. If they didn't buy, takes them through the sales promo. So we drop them into the same normal track that that we have for everyone else. It's just starts with the replay pro promo because they don't want to watch your webinar. One more piece. If they didn't attend the webinar, what do we do with them? Well, in that case, what we do is we again take them and we put them through the replay promo, which takes them through the rest of the sequence. So as you can see from this sequence, you have got a path that they go down if they attend, if they buy, if they don't buy, etc. And then you have another separate path for what happens if they don't register for the webinar, the one of the 70% who don't. And so that you can still continue to promote and sell to them. And very frankly, that's where the majority of the cells are that are going to come from your webinar funnel is going to come from that group of people that don't actually watch your webinar. Because you give them using this format a way to be able to find out more about your product and to eventually buy.
